brain_dumps.text: Grade 4 students are struggling with equivalent fractions. Need to create a visual worksheet using fraction tiles and number lines. Also quiz them on Friday to see where they stand.

actions.title: Equivalent Fractions Visual Worksheet
actions.description: Create a worksheet using fraction tiles and number lines for Grade 4 students struggling with equivalent fractions

actions.title: Equivalent Fractions Assessment
actions.description: Friday quiz to assess Grade 4 understanding of equivalent fractions

brain_dumps.text: Monday morning assembly ran long so Grade 1 Language Arts got cut short. Need to double up on sight words tomorrow. Aaron is making progress with his hearing support - the visual cue cards are working well. Should note that in his file.

actions.title: Extended Sight Words Lesson
actions.description: Double lesson on sight words for Grade 1 to make up for Monday's shortened class

actions.title: Update Aaron Williams IEP Notes
actions.description: Record progress with visual cue cards for hearing support accommodation

brain_dumps.text: Science fair is coming up in June. Grade 4 students need to start brainstorming project ideas around energy and simple machines. Maybe a cross-curricular project linking Science and Mathematics? Also should update the curriculum tracker - we covered energy transfer last week.

actions.title: Science Fair Prep: Energy and Simple Machines
actions.description: Cross-curricular project linking Grade 4 Science (energy/machines) with Mathematics for the science fair

actions.title: Mark Energy Transfer as Completed
actions.description: Update curriculum tracker - energy transfer topic covered last week in Grade 4 Science

brain_dumps.text: Term 2 midterms next week. Need a Grade 2 Math quiz on place value and addition with regrouping. Also need to prep a review worksheet for Grade 4 multiplication. Attendance has been low this week - check with parents of absent students.

actions.title: Grade 2 Place Value and Addition Quiz
actions.description: Midterm quiz covering place value and addition with regrouping for Grade 2

actions.title: Grade 4 Multiplication Review
actions.description: Review worksheet for long multiplication before midterm exams

actions.title: Follow Up on Absent Students
actions.description: Check with parents about student absences this week

brain_dumps.text: Great class today! Grade 1 students loved the counting song for addition facts. Need to plan more musical activities. Jade in Grade 2 had a rough day - her ADHD was really affecting her focus. The movement breaks helped but we might need to adjust the schedule for her.

actions.title: Musical Addition Activities for Grade 1
actions.description: Plan more musical and song-based activities for teaching addition facts to Grade 1

suggestions.text: Jade in Grade 2 had a rough day - her ADHD was really affecting her focus. The movement breaks helped but we might need to adjust the schedule for her.

brain_dumps.text: Need to create a rubric for the Grade 4 narrative writing assignment due next week. Students should be assessed on story structure, character development, grammar, and creativity. Also the Reading Comprehension unit is almost done - time to move to informational text.

actions.title: Narrative Writing Rubric - Grade 4
actions.description: Rubric for Grade 4 narrative writing covering story structure, character development, grammar, and creativity

actions.title: Complete Reading Comprehension Unit
actions.description: Mark Reading Comprehension Strategies as completed and begin Informational Text unit

brain_dumps.text: Parent of Samuel Victor called - wants to know how he is doing in English since he is an ELL student. Should prepare a progress summary. Also thinking about creating a presentation for the Term 1 parent night showing class progress across all three grades.

actions.title: Samuel Victor Progress Summary
actions.description: Prepare ELL progress summary for Samuel Victor's parent

actions.title: Term 1 Parent Night Presentation
actions.description: Create presentation showing class progress across Grade 1, 2, and 4 for parent night

brain_dumps.text: Grade 2 Science experiment on plant growth went really well. Students measured their bean plants and recorded data. Should create a follow-up worksheet connecting the measurements to the math we did on standard units. Priya needs the simplified text version as usual.

actions.title: Plant Growth Measurement Worksheet
actions.description: Follow-up worksheet connecting Grade 2 Science plant measurement data to Mathematics standard units

suggestions.text: Priya needs the simplified text version as usual.

brain_dumps.text: Independence Day is coming up on Feb 22. Want to do something special with all three grades - maybe a multigrade lesson on Saint Lucia's history and national symbols. Could tie in Language Arts with writing about what independence means to them.

actions.title: Independence Day Multigrade Lesson
actions.description: Multigrade lesson for Grades 1, 2, and 4 on Saint Lucia's history, national symbols, and independence

actions.title: Plan Independence Day Activities
actions.description: Coordinate Independence Day celebration activities across all grades

brain_dumps.text: End of term coming up. Need to grade the last batch of Grade 4 Science quizzes on ecosystems. Also should create a fun review game for Grade 1 Math - maybe a counting competition. The storybook project for Grade 1 Language Arts turned out great, students love The Mango Tree.

actions.title: Grade Ecosystems Quizzes
actions.description: Grade the final batch of Grade 4 Science quizzes on ecosystems

actions.title: Grade 1 Math Review Game
actions.description: Fun counting competition review game for Grade 1 Mathematics end of term

brain_dumps.text: Jounen Kweyol next week! Should incorporate Creole heritage into lessons. Grade 2 Science could study local plants and traditional uses. Grade 4 can write about Creole food culture in Language Arts. Need to check which students are performing in the cultural show.

actions.title: Local Plants and Traditional Uses
actions.description: Grade 2 Science lesson on local Saint Lucian plants and their traditional Creole uses for Jounen Kweyol

actions.title: Creole Food Culture Writing
actions.description: Grade 4 Language Arts lesson on writing about Creole food culture for Jounen Kweyol

suggestions.text: Need to check which students are performing in the cultural show.

brain_dumps.text: Final exams approaching. Grade 4 Math needs a comprehensive review covering fractions, multiplication, and division. Grade 2 needs a Science review on all topics covered. Should also prepare image resources for the exam study guides - diagrams for plant parts and the water cycle.

actions.title: Grade 4 Math Comprehensive Review
actions.description: End of year review worksheet covering fractions, multiplication, and division for Grade 4

actions.title: Grade 2 Science Review Quiz
actions.description: Comprehensive review quiz covering all Grade 2 Science topics for end of year

actions.title: Study Guide Diagrams
actions.description: Create plant parts and water cycle diagrams for Grade 2 Science exam study guides

milestones.subject: Mathematics
milestones.checklist_json:
[{"key": "0", "text": "1. Learners will be expected to: 
• Combine quantities (sets, lengths, etc.) using concrete materials to get the total or find a missing value up to 20.
• Partition and recombine quantities to relate to addition and subtraction.
• Explain how addition and subtraction are related.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "1", "text": "Add and subtract within 20, using a variety of strategies.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "2", "text": "Determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "3", "text": "Separate quantities to find a missing value from a number up to 20.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "4", "text": "Comparing two quantities to find the difference from a number up to 20", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5", "text": "Using symbols for addition (+) and subtraction (-) as a way to record calculations done through stories, building, and drawing.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6", "text": "Make reasonable estimation when combining, separating, comparing & partitioning quantities to 20.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "7", "text": "Model and describe equal collections of objects as ___ groups of ___.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "8", "text": "Form equal groups of objects and use repeated addition to represent the group.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "9", "text": "Skip count to find the total number of objects in groups.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "10", "text": "Write mathematical statements to represent groups of equal objects.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "11", "text": "Share objects into equal groups and state how many objects are in each group.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "12", "text": "Write mathematical statements to represent equal groups of objects shared.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-13T09:00:00Z"}]

(up to four digits) with and without regroupi", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-07T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "11", "text": "2. Mentally add 10, 100, or 1000 to any given number;", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-27T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "13", "text": "4. mentally add multiples of ten and hundred e.g. 70 + 20, 500 + 300, 4000 + 5000;", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "14", "text": "Include task cards with multi-digit addition and subtraction problems that require regrouping or borrowing.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-25T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "15", "text": "Students can use the manipulatives to represent the numbers and model the regrouping or borrowing process.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-25T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "16", "text": "Task Card Station: Provide task cards with a mix of addition and subtraction problems, including multi-digit numbers and regrouping or borrowing scenarios. Students can work independently or in pairs to solve the problems on the task cards.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "17", "text": "Technology Station: Set up computers or tablets with math fluency apps or online practice platforms that offer activities related to addition and subtraction. Choose apps or platforms that provide adaptive learning experiences and allow students to practice regrouping or borrowing concepts.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-12-03T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "18", "text": "True and False Statements: Have learners sort number sentences into true and false statements. Allow learners to explain and demonstrate why the statement is either true or false.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "19", "text": "Product \u2013 Addition/Subtraction number pattern: Distribute template to learners have them create card showing addition or subtraction of numbers with multiples of 10 and 100. Allow pupils to identify and explain the pattern shown on the card.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-21T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "20", "text": "2. Subtract 1,2, 3 digits numbers from multi-digits numbers mentally.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-12-04T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "21", "text": "Skill 3. Estimate the addition of 1, 2, 3, digit whole numbers with multi-digits.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "22", "text": "4. Estimate the subtraction of 1,2,3, digit whole numbers from multi -digit numbers.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-02T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "23", "text": "5. Round off whole numbers to the nearest tens hundreds and thousands.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-29T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "24", "text": "Values 6. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-23T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "25", "text": "Use strategies to recall multiplication and division facts up to 100.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-21T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "26", "text": "Represent multiplication concretely using a variety of materials (square tiles, two-sided counters, etc.) and base ten blocks;", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-24T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "27", "text": "Demonstrate the relationship between multiplication and division by using simple examples.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-26T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "28", "text": "Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to three-digit dividends and one-digit divisors.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-12-03T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "29", "text": "Use multiplication and division in a given equation", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-10-27T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "30", "text": "Volunteer to share drawings to represent given multiplication and division problems.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-27T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "31", "text": "Poster Creation: Create a poster to advertise the fundraiser event. Include the event name, date, time, location, and a brief description. Use colorful markers, crayons, or other art supplies to make your poster eye-catching and appealing.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "32", "text": "Calculation and Total: Calculate the total number of items needed for the fundraiser by solving the multiplication problem. Use the concrete materials to count the total number of items visually. Record the total number of items on your poster.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "33", "text": "Checklist for Assessment: Poster Creation: Poster includes all required information (event name, date, time, location, description). Poster is visually appealing and attention-grabbing.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-04T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "34", "text": "Multiplication Representation: Multiplication problem is clearly stated. Concrete materials are used to represent the problem accurately. Multiplication expression is labeled correctly.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "35", "text": "Calculation and Total: Multiplication problem is solved correctly. Total number of items is counted accurately using concrete materials. Total number of items is recorded clearly on the poster.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "36", "text": "Hands-on activities: Provide hands-on activities like using manipulatives such as counters, cubes, or even edible items like candies to demonstrate multiplication and division concepts. This can appeal to kinesthetic learners and make abstract concepts more tangible.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-12-01T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "37", "text": "1. Explain the concept of a unit rate associated with a ratio a:b, where b\u22600.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-26T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "38", "text": "2. Apply rate language effectively within the context of ratio relationships.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-11-02T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "39", "text": "3. Use unit rates in situations involving measurement conversions or rates of change.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-12-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "40", "text": "4. Demonstrate Part-Whole Relationships: understanding of part-whole relationships by partitioning and combining concrete materials to represent different ratios and their corresponding quantities, ensuring that the total remains consistent within the context of 100.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-12-10T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "41", "text": "5. Communicate their understanding of ratios clearly and precisely, both verbally and in writing, using appropriate mathematical language and notation to express ratio relationships and unit rates accurately.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2025-12-03T09:00:00Z"}]

"2026-01-29T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "3", "text": "Create and extend simple patterns with 3-5 elements.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "4", "text": "Create patterns resulting from repeating an operation or making a repeated change to an attribute.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5", "text": "Copy and extend a repeating pattern (using manipulatives, diagrams, body movements/actions/sounds)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-30T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6", "text": "Translate simple repeating patterns (E.g., a repeating orientation pattern to a repeating sound pattern, etc.).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "7", "text": "Copy and extend growing patterns.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "8", "text": "Recognize patterns as either repeating or growing.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "9", "text": "Acknowledge patterns in their environment by identifying and describing the pattern observed around them.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-23T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "10", "text": "Identify and enjoy patterns in poems, stories, (folk and modern) within their culture.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "11", "text": "Can students extend given patterns involving shapes, colour and sizes?", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "12", "text": "Students should be able to identify a pattern as growing or repeating.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-30T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "13", "text": "Students should be able to extend growing patterns.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-31T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "14", "text": "Can students explain the pattern rule used? e.g. 2, 4,6,8,10,", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-27T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "15", "text": "Johnny did this with his building blocks", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "16", "text": "Tina wrote these numbers down on her book 5, 10, 15, 20, 5, 10, 15, 20", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-31T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "17", "text": "Determine the rule used to extend a growing and a shrinking pattern.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-22T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "18", "text": "Identify, describe, and create through investigation, growing and shrinking patterns (increasing and decreasing) involving addition and subtraction.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-25T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "19", "text": "Skills: Solve simple patterns involving increasing and decreasing patterns.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-28T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "20", "text": "Use pattern rules to extend patterns and to make predictions.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "21", "text": "Complete patterns where elements are missing.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-25T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "22", "text": "Values: Demonstrate the ability to obtain a rule for a number pattern by determining the relationship between given numbers.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-06T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "23", "text": "Identify and interpret simple number sentences using words, diagrams and symbols.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-08T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "24", "text": "Communicate and interpret simple additive and subtractive strategies using words, diagrams and symbols.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-08T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "25", "text": "Explain how the arrangement of numbers (commutative property) does not affect the sum/total.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-30T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "26", "text": "Recognize that the commutative property does not apply to subtraction.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "27", "text": "Represent algebraic expressions using variables and symbols.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-30T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "28", "text": "Represent unknown quantities concretely, pictorially and numerically.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-05T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "29", "text": "Simplify algebraic expression problems involving an unknown quantity in an addition and subtraction situation.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-03T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "30", "text": "Demonstrate an appreciation for the use of symbols as a substitute for real life objects when counting and grouping items up to a hundred.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-23T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "31", "text": "Simplify these simple algebraic expressions using one operation at a time. 2f + f, h + 4h, 5a - 2a, 6b - 4b", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-02T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "32", "text": "Represent numbers up to 100 in a variety of ways.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "33", "text": "Apply the commutative property when add numbers.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "34", "text": "Identifying equivalent monetary values of coins and bills.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-30T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "35", "text": "Create equivalent sum of money using different coins.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-23T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "36", "text": "Generalize whole numbers can be decomposed and composed without changing its value.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-05T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "37", "text": "Record equalities and inequalities symbolically using the correct symbol.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-08T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "38", "text": "Solve for missing quantities to make the equation equivalent.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-26T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "39", "text": "Model equalities using a variety of concrete representation and record.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-03T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "40", "text": "Appreciate the need for equating an expression to values.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "41", "text": "Accept that an equation is a number sentence.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-01-11T09:00:00Z"}]

"2026-02-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.23", "text": "produce fiction, non-fiction, poetry, and visual drafts that incorporate content from other core areas", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "94", "text": "- use of descriptive language", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "95", "text": "- use of dialogue", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "96", "text": "Identify 3 facts from the text", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "97", "text": "Write 2 interesting information you would like to share from the text", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "98", "text": "Write 1 question you have about any information from the text", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "99", "text": "Complete a T-Chart based on a narrative text read or viewed.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "100", "text": "Learners create their written responses to text read.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "101", "text": "read and view to develop understanding of how vocabulary and language use influence the meaning and mood of the text", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "102", "text": "write and represent, using organizational strategies to arrange thoughts to explore, clarify and reflect on ideas, feelings, and experiences", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "103", "text": "write and represent to create a written or representative draft", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "104", "text": "I would like to be better at ...", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "105", "text": "I would like to invent ...", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "106", "text": "2) Understand author\u2019s style: Understanding an author's style enriches the reading experience by deepening comprehension, fostering critical thinking skills, and cultivating a deeper appreciation for literature as a form of artistic expression.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "107", "text": "3) Compare and contrast fiction and nonfiction: By understanding the distinct characteristics of fiction and nonfiction, students will gain deeper insight into the different ways in which authors craft their writing to inform, entertain and persuade readers.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "108", "text": "4) Character Development: Teaching character development in reading enriches the reading experience, promotes critical thinking and empathy, fosters cultural understanding, and empowers readers to engage more deeply with literature.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "109", "text": "5) Knowledge of implied and explicit messages/morals through a critical lens (moral of the story, theme): Understanding implied and explicit messages involves analyzing the text to uncover deeper meanings, themes, and moral lessons.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-15T09:00:00Z"}]

milestones.checklist_json: [{"key": "0", "text": "Strengthen problem-solving skills by recognizing patterns that lead to faster and more reliable solutions.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "1", "text": "To skip count you add the same number over and over.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "2", "text": "We count by fives quite often; counting money and telling the time are two examples. The picture below shows how to count forward by fives starting from 30.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "3", "text": "The two examples below show counting by tens. Look at the numbers. There is always a pattern.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "4", "text": "The examples below show counting by hundreds. The patterns in all two are the same; the number in the hundreds place goes up by one each time.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5", "text": "Knowledge:", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6", "text": "1. Recognize the importance of patterns in solving multiplication and division problems.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-10T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "33", "text": "Can you think of any real-life situations where knowing how to multiply or divide by 10, 100, or 1,000 would be useful?", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "34", "text": "Find a partner and share your thoughts on the questions above. Discuss your understanding of place value patterns and how it relates to multiplication and division by 10, 100, and 1,000.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "35", "text": "Take turns listening to each other and ask clarifying questions if needed.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "36", "text": "Each pair will take turn sharing their thoughts and findings with the whole class.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "37", "text": "As a class, they will discuss some real-life situations where knowing how to multiply or divide by 10, 100, or 1,000 would be useful.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "38", "text": "They will also discuss any challenges or insights they gained from this activity.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "39", "text": "Fill in the missing number: 8 + ___ = 15 a) 5 b) 6 c) 7 d) 9", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "40", "text": "Fill in the missing number: 18 - ___ = 9 a) 5 b) 6 c) 7 d) 8", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "41", "text": "Fill in the missing number: ___ + 12 = 20 - 5 a) 7 b) 8 c) 9 d) 10", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "42", "text": "Fill in the missing number: 23 - ___ = 15 + 3 a) 3 b) 5 c) 8 d) 12", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "43", "text": "Fill in the missing number: ___ + 6 + 13 = 31 a) 4 b) 6 c) 7 d) 10", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-10T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "44", "text": "Match the following vocabulary terms with their definitions: 1. How might this skill be useful in real-life situations?", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "48", "text": "Each member of Group A will solve one multiplication problem from the set independently, filling in the blank space with the correct number.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "49", "text": "Once everyone has completed their problem, they will come together as a group and discuss their solutions.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "50", "text": "As a group, they will identify the open sentences in the multiplication problems and explain how they solved them.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "51", "text": "They will create a summary of their findings to share with the other groups.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "52", "text": "Each member of Group B will receive a multiplication problem with an open sentence.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "53", "text": "1. Recognize when two expressions are equal and when they are not equal in addition, subtraction, multiplication and division using mathematical language", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "54", "text": "2. Calculate the operations to determine whether they are equal and unequal", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "58", "text": "6. Share to ensure the equality is preserved", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "59", "text": "7. true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "58", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding that large objects/structures are made up of smaller parts/components/units.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "59", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding that objects may be taken to pieces/", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "60", "text": "disassembled into their component parts.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "61", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding that small pieces can be assembled to form larger structures.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "62", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding that new objects can be formed from the pieces of a disassembled object/structure.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "63", "text": "Explain how objects can be built up, torn down and reassembled using the same parts.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "64", "text": "Account for any differences that they may observe between the disassembled object to the new object made from its pieces.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "65", "text": "Account for the differences between the disassembled object and the new object made from its pieces.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "66", "text": "Distinguish between the components of the disassembled object and the components of new objects.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "67", "text": "State that matter is made up of combinations of smaller pieces (atoms) that can be combined in many different ways.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "68", "text": "Observe objects to determine the various parts they are made up of.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "69", "text": "Observe how an object made of a small set of pieces can be disassembled and made into a new object.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "70", "text": "Manipulate an object made from a set of small pieces by disassembling it and assembling a new object.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "71", "text": "Compare and contrast the characteristics of the original structure with the new structure made from the same materials in terms of its shape, properties, uses, etc.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "72", "text": "Communicate to the class and teacher, the results from various investigations done on assembling, disassembling and reassembling different objects.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "73", "text": "Interpret results obtained from various investigations done on assembling, disassembling and reassembling different objects.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "74", "text": "act as building blocks for many other things.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "75", "text": "Show persistence in the assembling, disassembling and reassembling of different objects/structures.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "76", "text": "Use available evidence in determining how to assemble objects or assemble pieces to make new objects.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "77", "text": "Respect the environment and use resources wisely.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "78", "text": "When conducting practical work, help group members/ classmates who may have difficulty in manipulating different pieces.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "79", "text": "Develop interest and curiosity by conducting investigations to gather evidence.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "80", "text": "Use inquiry to investigate and solve problems related to assembling and disassembling of objects.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "81", "text": "Respect the evidence gathered through their investigations.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "82", "text": "Use their inventiveness to conceptualize and construct objects from small sets of pieces.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "83", "text": "Follow all recommended safety protocols, including: Wear safety goggles when conducting certain experiments. Be careful when pulling objects apart or when putting pieces together to form new structures.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "84", "text": "Collaborate with classmates in assembling, disassembling and reassembling of different objects/structures.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "85", "text": "Participate actively in classroom discussions.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "86", "text": "Define the terms: Wilt, Freeze, Kernel, Heating, Steam, Cooling, Reversible, Freezing, Melting", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "87", "text": "Compare substances before and after adding and/or removing heat.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "88", "text": "Account for why some processes are reversible and some are not.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "89", "text": "Explain the effect of heating and cooling on substances.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "90", "text": "Distinguish between a reversible and an irreversible change.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "91", "text": "Become aware of the dangers of irreversible change for farming and the possible precautions a grower can take.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "92", "text": "State that water can change from solid to liquid and from liquid to solid (ST-2-PS-MM-7).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "93", "text": "Describe the conditions for state change of water (ST-2-PS-MM-8).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "94", "text": "Identify with examples, physical change in everyday life E.g. water (ST-3-PS-MM-2).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "95", "text": "Practice the use of new vocabulary.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "96", "text": "Observe the effect of adding and/ or removing heat from different substances.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "97", "text": "Interpret the results of teacher demonstrations. Make hypothesis about findings.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "98", "text": "Classify changes as reversible or irreversible.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "99", "text": "Record their observations after heat is applied to different substances.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "100", "text": "Communicate their ideas in discussions.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "101", "text": "Predict the effects of heat on solids and liquids.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "102", "text": "Demonstrate an awareness that irreversible changes in agriculture can be detrimental to humans.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "103", "text": "Appreciate that reversible and irreversible changes from applying or removing heat takes place in our everyday life.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "104", "text": "Display Interest/Curiosity on the importance/use of heat in the home.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "105", "text": "Engage in Inquiry to determine the effects of heat on substances.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "106", "text": "Show respect for evidence collected from engaging in scientific inquiry.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "107", "text": "Work cooperatively and collaboratively in groups.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "108", "text": "Practice safety when applying heat to substances.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "109", "text": "Display sensitivity and offer assistance to peers who may have physical or learning challenges when conducting practical and group work.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "110", "text": "Participate actively in classroom discussions.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "111", "text": "A reversible change happens when no new materials have been created.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "112", "text": "With reversible changes, there are no new materials added.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "113", "text": "Some examples of reversible changes include: ice changing to liquid water, a chocolate bar turning to liquid chocolate, a sheet of paper cut into small pieces.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "114", "text": "In each example, no new material/substance is made.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "115", "text": "Any object that cannot be changed to its original state is called irreversible change.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "116", "text": "Irreversible changes happen when a new substance or material is created.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "117", "text": "The original material or substance cannot be obtained from the new item.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "118", "text": "Some examples include: baking a cake, frying an egg, making jam, burning paper, making charcoal.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "119", "text": "When some materials are cooled they also go through reversible changes: ice to water, freezing melted chocolate.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "120", "text": "When heat is added to materials/objects, they can go through a reversible or irreversible change.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "121", "text": "Some substances expand (get bigger) when they are heated while others contract (get smaller).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "122", "text": "There may also be a change in the colour of the substance and the amount of the substance (volume).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "123", "text": "We must always ensure that we are safe when adding heat to any material.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "124", "text": "This should not be done without the help of an adult.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "125", "text": "Wear protective gear and maintain safe distances.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "126", "text": "Use the correct tools for working with heating appliances and handling hot items.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "127", "text": "DO NOT PLAY with FIRE!", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}]

milestones.subject: Mathematics
milestones.checklist_json: [{"key": "0", "text": "Explore physical materials and describe them using the vocabulary associated with linear measure (short/long, high/low, near/far, thick/thin, narrow/wide, deep/shallow).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "1", "text": "Classify the measuring attribute of length based on the vocabulary associated with it.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "2", "text": "Categorise the measuring attribute of capacity based on the vocabulary associated with it.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "3", "text": "Categorise the measuring attribute of mass based on the vocabulary associated with it.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "4", "text": "Categorise the measuring attribute of area based on the vocabulary associated with it.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-16T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5", "text": "To culminate the measuring attribute, length, a graphic organizer will be created with learners with all the describing words/vocabulary learnt about how long or short objects and distances are. A discussion will bring out the measuring attribute of length.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6", "text": "Compare the length of two objects using direct comparison (i.e: place side by side and align one end) and explain reasoning using appropriate vocabulary; (shorter/longer, higher/lower, deeper/shallower, nearer/farther, thinner/ thicker, wider and narrower).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "7", "text": "Compare the mass of two objects (lifting, pushing, pulling and balancing) and explain reasoning using appropriate vocabulary;", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "8", "text": "Compare the capacity of two objects (filling and emptying) and explain reasoning using appropriate vocabulary (such as more or less, level is higher than or less than).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "9", "text": "Compare the area of two objects using direct comparison(i.e", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "10", "text": "which one occupies more space) and explain reasoning using appropriate vocabulary (such as bigger and smaller).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-17T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "11", "text": "Example: You would not use a hand span to measure capacity.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "12", "text": "Values: Show enthusiasm using non-standard measurement to measure objects.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "13", "text": "List examples when they estimate measurement in their everyday lives.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-19T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "14", "text": "Appreciate the importance of using estimations in everyday situations.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "15", "text": "Be willing to share the date every morning in class.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-18T09:00:00Z"}]

Create real life scenarios that involve measuring lengths, height and distances.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-05T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "25", "text": "Aligns the beginning of the object with the zero point and reads the measurement at the end of the object.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-27T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "26", "text": "Height Measurement: Measures the height of objects accurately, using the same principles as length measurement.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-07T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "27", "text": "Width Measurement: Measures the width of objects accurately, using the same principles as length measurement.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-06T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "28", "text": "Picture Measurement: Measures the height, length, and width of given pictures accurately, using appropriate measuring tools and units.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "29", "text": "Recording Measurements: Records measurements neatly and correctly in the appropriate units (cm, in, or non-standard units).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-25T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "30", "text": "Comparing Measurements: Demonstrates an understanding of comparative measurement by comparing lengths, heights, and widths of different objects or pictures.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "31", "text": "Estimation: Makes reasonable estimates before measuring to develop a sense of measurement.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "32", "text": "Problem Solving: Applies measurement skills to solve simple problems or real-life scenarios, such as finding the total length of multiple objects or determining the size difference between two pictures.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-26T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "33", "text": "Open-Ended Tasks: Include open-ended tasks that allow for creativity and critical thinking. Design a poster on measuring everyday solids", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "39", "text": "Heaviness: Heaviness refers to the amount of force exerted by gravity on an object.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "40", "text": "Objects with greater mass tend to feel heavier because they experience a stronger gravitational pull.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "41", "text": "When comparing objects, the one with more mass is typically considered heavier.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "42", "text": "Lightness: Lightness refers to the perception of an object being less heavy or having less mass.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "43", "text": "Objects with lower mass feel lighter because they experience a weaker gravitational pull.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "44", "text": "Lightness is relative and can vary based on individual perception and the context of comparison.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "45", "text": "Comparing Weight: When comparing the weight of objects, consider their mass relative to each other.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "46", "text": "Use terms like "heavier than," "lighter than," or "about the same weight as" to make comparisons.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "47", "text": "Estimating Weight: Practice estimating the weight of objects by considering their size, material, and density.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "48", "text": "Develop a sense of weight through experience and observation.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "49", "text": "Skills: 2. a. Find the mass of objects by counting weighted units/objects, improvised units", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "50", "text": "2. b Find the mass of objects by using concrete materials (cubes, counters, etc.) (nonstandard method). (To be balanced: as it refers to Mass/Weight means equal.)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "51", "text": "Values: 3. can measure the temperature.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "113", "text": "Measure the temperature of frozen water, water and boiling water.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "114", "text": "Label a diagram of the water cycle.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "115", "text": "Use fists to demonstrate particle distance in solids, liquids and gas.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "116", "text": "Use fist model to explain the processes in the water cycle (evaporation, condensation, precipitation).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "117", "text": "Debate the importance of water conservation.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "118", "text": "Apply the reuse, recycle and reduce model to community improvement.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "119", "text": "Observe the evaporation and condensation of water (ST-3-ESS-EW-6).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "120", "text": "Collect and compare measurements of rainfall, temperature, wind direction and windspeed during a specific period (ST-2-ESS-EW-7).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "121", "text": "Use a thermometer to measure temperature and, explain how the device works (ST-3-ESS-EW-9).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "122", "text": "Appreciation: Students will develop an appreciation for the importance of water as a precious natural resource and recognize the need for conservation efforts.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "123", "text": "Interest/Curiosity: Students will develop a deep interest and curiosity in the topic of water by exploring and investigating its properties, distribution, and importance to living things.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "124", "text": "Inventiveness: Students will use their creativity and inventiveness to develop and implement solutions to real-world water-related problems, such as water pollution, scarcity, and distribution.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "125", "text": "Active Participation: Students will participate actively in classroom discussions related to water, developing critical thinking and communication skills through classroom debates, discussions and presentations about water.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "126", "text": "When conducting practical and group work, display", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "127", "text": "sensitivity and offer assistance to peers who may have physical or learning challenges.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "128", "text": "True or False: Write T if the statement is true or F if it is false. \u2022 We don\u2019t need water. (F) \u2022 People should use water wisely. (T) \u2022 Keeping the tap opened while brushing our teeth is good. (F)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "129", "text": "\u2022 Reusing and recycling things can reduce water pollution. (T) \u2022 Life would not be possible without water. (T)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "130", "text": "Water conservation: Look at the pictures. What message are they trying to explain? (Water is important, don\u2019t waste it, we should save water)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "131", "text": "Water Pollution worksheet: Identify the different items that add to water pollution in the worksheet.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "132", "text": "Teacher may work with the children to write a poem or song entitled \u201cWater, Water Everywhere\u201d that emphasizes the new vocabulary.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}]

milestones.checklist_json: [{"key": "0", "text": "Define the terms: Igneous rock, Sedimentary rock, Metamorphic rock, Weathering, Minerals, Erosion, Deposition, Fossils, Slate, Granite", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-23T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "1", "text": "Limestone, Marble", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-08T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "2", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding of how layers of rocks are formed (layers and heating processes).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-03T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "3", "text": "Recognise how rock formations result in the changes in the landscape.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "4", "text": "Create a representation of a canyon to illustrate how a river changes the landscape overtime.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5", "text": "Illustrate changes in the landscape.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-21T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6", "text": "Outline how soils are made from a variety of rocks. (ST 3 ESS ER 3)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-23T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "7", "text": "Collect and classify rocks in their country. (ST 4 ESS ER 1)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-23T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "8", "text": "Describe with examples how people use rocks for different purposes. (ST 4 ESS ER 2)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "9", "text": "Define the term soil erosion, listing the types of soil erosion, and ways and means of preventing it. (ST 5 ESS ER 1)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-05T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "10", "text": "Investigate how layers of rocks are formed.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "11", "text": "Observe how fossils in layers of rocks and rock formations indicate a change in the landscape.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-21T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "12", "text": "Communicate effectively how landscape changes", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-06T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "13", "text": "Interpret what fossils and rock formations may suggest about past environments.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-22T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "14", "text": "Research information about landscape changes caused by glaciers and volcanoes.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-09T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "15", "text": "Express desire to find answers in patterns through exploring.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-27T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "16", "text": "Willingly work with others, regardless of their age, their gender or physical or cultural characteristics.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-21T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "17", "text": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-25T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "20", "text": "Show concern and present community solutions to the problems of deforestation which leads to erosion, and eventually global earthquakes and volcanoes", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-11T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "43", "text": "Explain the concept of continental drift.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-01T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "44", "text": "Relay the evidence and logic that continental drift of the tectonic plates is a viable theory.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "45", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding of the use of the terms tectonic plate continent, ocean, mountain range, volcano, earthquake, ocean trench, ocean floor, etc. by using drawings and descriptions", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-12T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "46", "text": "Explain that mountains, volcanoes, earthquakes and ocean trenches are formed because of the movement of tectonic plates.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-21T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "47", "text": "Explain how the knowledge of the location of mountains, earthquakes and volcanoes affects the lives of people.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-20T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "48", "text": "Describe an earthquake as a natural occurrence and explain what causes it. (ST 5 LS ECS 15)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-02-26T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "49", "text": "Identify volcanic activity as a natural process and explain how volcanoes are formed, as well as the likely impact on the environment. (ST 6 LS ECS 16)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-04T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "51", "text": "Describe the safety measures to be put in practice during an earthquake, explaining the need for each measure. (ST 6 LS ECS 17)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-18T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "52", "text": "Explain how volcanoes are formed and discuss the impact that these eruptions cause. (ST 6 LS ECS 18)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-25T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "53", "text": "Outline useful and harmful effects of the presence of a volcano in their", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-05T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "54", "text": "Draw diagrams or formulate models to represent some features of the earth, e.g, an ocean floor, continents, etc.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-03-02T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "55", "text": "Predict locations where earthquakes or volcanoes could take place.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "56", "text": "Use crayons/markers/seeds to locate features of the earth on a blank map, e.g. continents, mountain ranges, volcanoes, trenches, etc.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "57", "text": "Draw diagrams that show that you understand three ways plate boundaries can interact (divergent, convergent and transform).", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "58", "text": "Account for how plate interactions can create mountains and furthermore cause earthquakes and volcanoes.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "59", "text": "Identify mountains and/or volcanoes in your country and account for why volcanic activity is prevalent in the Caribbean region.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "60", "text": "Effectively communicate information based on observation of patterns of the earth\u2019s features", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "61", "text": "Actively participate in class discussions.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "62", "text": "When conducting group work, display sensitivity and offer assistance to peers who may have physical or learning challenges", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "63", "text": "Work respectfully with each other while analysing maps and charts and videos to describe patterns of earth\u2019s features.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "64", "text": "Develop awareness that earth events and changes in earth\u2019s feature can affect the environment and the lives and habitats of living things", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "65", "text": "Learners are expected to: Knowledge: Define the terms: Earthquake, Flood, Volcanic eruption, Tsunami, Hurricane, Mudslides, Risk assessment, Risk reduction. Describe causes of Earthquakes, Hurricanes, floods, tsunamis, volcanic eruptions, mudslides", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "66", "text": "Describe mechanisms of Earthquakes, Hurricanes, floods, tsunamis, volcanic eruptions, mudslides", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "67", "text": "Assess risks associated with natural earth processes (hurricanes, floods, tsunami and earthquakes)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "68", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding of monitoring systems used for detection of different natural earth processes", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "69", "text": "Explain the significance of early warning signals in reducing the impact of natural earth processes", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "70", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding of structural factors that contribute to the stability of buildings", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "71", "text": "Apply understanding of structural principles to ensure that buildings can withstand seismic forces", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "72", "text": "Describes methods of reducing flooding and the impacts of flooding", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "73", "text": "Apply understanding of structural principles to designing of buildings that are able to withstand the force of a tsunami", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "74", "text": "Describe methods of reducing the impacts of mudslides", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "75", "text": "Describe an earthquake as a natural occurrence and explain what causes it. (ST 5 LS ECS 15)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "76", "text": "Identify ways in which earthquakes impact the environment. (ST 6 LS ECS 16)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "77", "text": "Describe the safety measures to be put in practice during an earthquake, explaining the need for each measure. (ST 6 LS ECS 17)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "78", "text": "Explain how volcanoes are formed and discuss the impact that these eruptions cause. (ST 6 LS ECS 18)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "79", "text": "Outline useful and harmful effects of the presence of a volcano in their environment. (ST 6 LS ECS 19)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "80", "text": "Brainstorm with peers the ways to reduce the impact or prepare for forces of nature such as hurricanes, volcanoes, mudslides etc.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "81", "text": "Identify the problems in their community with respect to the natural forces.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "82", "text": "Problem solve solutions to the challenges that arise form natural forces.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "83", "text": "Secure feedback from leaders of the community on how to prepare for and mitigate problems.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "84", "text": "Predict how a building\u2019s design would affect its ability to withstand the effect of natural earth processes", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "85", "text": "Observe and identify features of buildings that make them resilient to natural earth process", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "86", "text": "Design a simple earth-resistant structure using everyday material", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "87", "text": "Design a simple structure that would be appropriate for a tsunami prone area", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "88", "text": "Develop a sense of concern for persons affected by natural earth processes", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "89", "text": "Appreciate the importance of developing resilient structures that can withstand the effects of natural earth processes", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "90", "text": "Appreciate the importance of paying attention to early warning signals so as to reduce the impact of natural earth processes", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "91", "text": "Value practices that lead to flood prevention", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "92", "text": "Recognize the importance of teamwork milestones.subject: Language Arts
milestones.checklist_json: [{"key": "5.1", "text": "Begin to develop strategies for the prewriting stages of the writing process", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.2", "text": "Talk about the ideas they plan to write about; provide learners with prompts that are of interest and relevant", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.3", "text": "Draft some illustrations to accompany ideas and write thoughts that match the ideas in their drawings", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.4", "text": "Use role play to assist with brainstorming ideas", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.5", "text": "Begin to use simple graphic organizers to brainstorm and plan", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.6", "text": "Begin to become familiar with the purpose of various genres and how different genres look and sound: narratives, poetry, expository", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.7", "text": "Begin to develop strategies for the connecting prewriting to the drafting stages of the writing process", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.8", "text": "Write to match text to their brainstorm, planning and drawings", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.9", "text": "Begin to identify the topic, purpose, audience and form for writing", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.10", "text": "Begin to experiment with creating a draft in different forms e.g. poems, invitations, excuses, informal letters", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.11", "text": "Begin to experiment with writing in simple sentences that may include descriptive words or simple transition words (and, but, then etc.).", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.12", "text": "Begin to organise sentences into paragraphs by writing narratives with two or more appropriately sequenced events and some sense of closure", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.13", "text": "With assistance, begin to become familiar with the keyboard for individual or shared writing", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.14", "text": "Work with a partner, in small groups and independently, to create a draft writing in both print and/or digital format", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.15", "text": "Continue to use role plays to assist with drafting process planning, convey and enhance meaning", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "5.16", "text": "Apply lessons learned about writing to record feelings, thoughts and ideas during Independent or Journal writing time.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "16", "text": "Use organisational strategies to arrange thoughts to explore,", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.1", "text": "engage in teacher assisted and peer collaboration to review the organisation of the written draft through the process of ARMS: Add, Remove, Move, Substitute", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.2", "text": "revisit brainstorming and illustration and illustration to add details to and/or delete details.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.3", "text": "Reread their draft and begin to recognize where they can make changes to better organise the beginning, middle, and end; first, second third, etc.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.4", "text": "Experiment with developing writing with a sense of flow", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.5", "text": "Begin to establish a personal voice in their writing by using pictures and words that convey their attitude or feelings towards the subject or audience", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.6", "text": "Experiment with using multi-sensory words (hearing, smell), taste, etc.) to improve writing", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.7", "text": "Begin to use comparison words to distinguish one thing from another\u2014size, shape, texture", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.8", "text": "Become comfortable making decisions about when to include Home Language and/ or Standard English", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.9", "text": "Develop more understanding of how and when to use descriptive or action words to extend meaning of draft", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.10", "text": "apply knowledge of spoken language to know when to use pronouns, plurals, and words indicating position (above, below, under, etc.)", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.11", "text": "experiment with some variety in first sentences (leads) of writing", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "6.12", "text": "demonstrate understanding of the connection between word order and meaningful writing", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "6.13", "text": "Begin to use various types of sentences: declarative, question, and exclamation", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "6.14", "text": "Use techniques demonstrated by favorite authors (descriptive words, funny words, short sentences, long sentence, asking questions, etc.)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "6.16", "text": "Use expressive language to elaborate and create interest", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "32", "text": "Write for pleasure and personal growth: use the writing process to share ideas with peers; identify personal strengths as writers and search for new ways to explore ideas", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.1", "text": "Develop increased confidence, and accuracy willingness to try out various strategies (risk taking) to increase spelling and punctuation accuracy, tidiness and presentation", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.2", "text": "Write from left to right and from top to bottom consistently", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.3", "text": "Use some conventional spacing", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.4", "text": "Continue to develop a relaxed hand while writing", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.5", "text": "Attempt to make letter formation readable by various audiences", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.6", "text": "Monitor and check high frequency words with the aid of:", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.8", "text": "Continue to develop spelling of single syllable words by applying knowledge of: - beginning and ending consonants - two-letter initial consonant blends - beginning and some ending consonant digraphs - short and long vowel sounds - knowledge of vowel clusters", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.9", "text": "Become increasingly familiar with predictable letter patterns to spell more accurately: - CVC (consonant, vowel, consonant)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.10", "text": "Apply letter and word knowledge strategies during the writing process to monitor (notice errors), edit errors and check for correctness.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.11", "text": "Apply sight word knowledge gathered through reading and writing to \u201ctry out\u201d different spellings of a word to see which one looks right.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.12", "text": "Begin to be aware of digital features such as spell check", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.13", "text": "Spell commonly used sight words and phonetically regular words with increasing correctness in final copies", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.14", "text": "Begin to add a few grade-level punctuation conventions: -full stops -question marks -exclamation marks -capitals on names and at the beginning of a sentence.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7.15", "text": "Develop hand created or digital presentation styles using various forms of hand or digitally generated: - illustrations - titles - fonts", "checked": false, "checked_at": construct 2D shapes and 3D objects.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "13", "text": "Skill: Sort 2D shapes and 3D objects based on their attributes and Acknowledge the importance of 3D and 2D shapes in the creation of various objects in the environment.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "33", "text": "Identify the shapes with the apex as having one base.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "34", "text": "Group shapes on the premise of having two identical bases.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "35", "text": "Recognise triangular shapes all around a group of shapes.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "36", "text": "Recognise rectangular shapes all around a group of shapes.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "37", "text": "Correctly name the groups as prisms and pyramids.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "38", "text": "Vertices: A vertex is the corner where edges meet.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "39", "text": "Some examples of 3D shapes and their properties in terms of number of edges, vertices and faces.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "40", "text": "Cylinder: A cylinder is a tubed shaped object. It consists of two circular bases joined by a curved face. A Cylinder has two curved edges and no vertices because it is made up of curved surfaces.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "41", "text": "Spheres: A round ball shape solid. It has no surface, edge or vertex.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-14T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "42", "text": "Pyramid: A solid in which the base is a polygon and all lateral faces are triangles that meet at a point called an apex. They are often named based on the bases that make the pyramid.", "checked": true, "checked_at": "2026-04-13T09:00:00Z"}, {"key": "43", "text": "Prism: A solid with two identical polygonal bases and whose edges are joined by rectangular faces. Prisms are often named according to the kind of polygon that makes the base of the prism. For example, a prism with a triangular base is called a triangular prism.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "44", "text": "Some examples of 2D polygons and their properties in terms of number of corners and sides", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "45", "text": "1. A triangle is made up of three sides and three corners.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "47", "text": "3. A square is made up of sides of the same length and 4 corners.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "48", "text": "4. A rectangle is made up of 4 sides in which opposite sides are equal in length and 4 corners.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "49", "text": "5. A pentagon is made up of 5 sides and 5 corners.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "50", "text": "Key Skills/ Concept: Observing, recognizing, comparing 2-D shapes, 3-D objects", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "51", "text": "2. Use a variety of models, materials to create shapes.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "54", "text": "5. milestones.checklist_json: [{"key": "5.1", "text": "Maintain a personal record of interests, personal or community experiences, music, books, pictures for use as writing prompts.", "checked": false, milestones.checklist_json: [{"key": "0", "text": "Learners are expected to: Knowledge - Define the terms: Weather, Weather forecasting, meteorologist/meteorology, temperature, precipitation, wind direction, clouds, moisture/humidity, climate, atmosphere.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "1", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding of the formation of clouds.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "2", "text": "Review and explain the components and processes involved in the water cycle.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "3", "text": "Distinguish between weather and climate.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "4", "text": "Identify and describe weather patterns at a particular time at a given time.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "5", "text": "Identify instruments used to measure the elements of weather.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "6", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding of how to use a) thermometer to measure the degree of hotness and coldness, b) anemometer to measure wind speed, c) rain gauge to measure the amount of rainfall, d) windvane to measure wind direction.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7", "text": "Skills - Identify types of weather conditions by examining weather charts. (ST 3 ESS EW 10)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "8", "text": "Investigate and report from data collected about the weather over a period, using simple graphs. milestones.subject: Social Studies

(ST 2 STSE 2)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "114", "text": "Demonstrate awareness that humans must realise that in their attempts to understand the world and exploit the environment to fulfil their needs there are consequences. (ST 2 STSE 1)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "115", "text": "Demonstrate awareness that the real impact of scientific and technological activities may take years to be seen. (ST 4 STSE 2)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "116", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding that human production processes make goods and products which may impact the environment. (ST 5 PS MM 4)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "117", "text": "Demonstrate an understanding that humans desire to acquire understanding and to produce materials for their needs, and there may be consequences not anticipated. (ST 5 TE UT 1)", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "118", "text": "Appreciate that the earth\u2019s water and air resources are sensitive to the abuse of humans.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "119", "text": "Demonstrate interest/curiosity to sample human habits like water consumption.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "120", "text": "Demonstrate an attitude of inquiry into new technologies that might impact the overall health of the earth.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}]
