the writer\u2019s purpose and a conclusion to restate the purpose", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "5.20", "text": "Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and "5.22", "text": "Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": and audiences.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "25", "text": "Write and represent to create a written or representative draft", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "6.1", Create real life problems involving ratios", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "46", "text": "Create a Ratio Story: Have learners write a story that involves ratios and include questions that require their peers to convert those ratios into fractions", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "47", "text": "Guided Practice with Step-by-Step Scaffolding: If 25% of a class is 10 learners, learners will solve for the total number of learners in the class using the formula: Whole=10/25\u00d7100=40 = Thus, the whole class has 40 learners.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "48", "text": "Learners are expected to: Knowledge 1. Use different methods to convert percentages to decimals and fractions, and vice versa.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "52", "text": "5. Create and interpret visual models of percentages, such as pie charts, bar graphs, and 100 grids.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "53", "text": "6. Explain reasoning and approach to solving percentage problems, both in writing and orally.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "54", "text": "Values 7. milestones.subject: Mathematics
milestones.checklist_json: [{"key": "0", "text": "Values: 10. Apply the pattern rules they have learned to solve real-world problems. 11. Create real-life problem that will utilize pattern rules to solve the problem.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "1", "text": "Values - Justify the relevance of patterns/rules in real-life situations.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "2", "text": "Learners are expected to: Knowledge - Identify and construct expressions/equations with unknowns from given situations", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "3", "text": "Skills - Write and solve problems with expressions/equations with unknowns for addition, subtraction, multiplication, and or division", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "4", "text": "Values - Identify and solve a real- life situation with expression/equations using unknowns for any of the four operations.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "5", "text": "Learners are expected to: Knowledge 1. Identify whether two expressions using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division with whole numbers are equal or unequal. Justify your conclusion by applying operations properties, including commutative, associative, and distributive.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "6", "text": "2. Add and subtract decimals up to the thousandth place and compare the results to determine if two decimal expressions are equal or unequal.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "7", "text": "Skills 3. Compare fractions by finding common denominators or using visual fraction models to determine if they are equal or unequal and explain their reasoning.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "8", "text": "4. Use measurement formulae (such as area and perimeter) to calculate and compare the quantities of two objects to determine if they are equal or unequal.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "9", "text": "5. Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators by finding equivalent fractions and determining if the results of two expressions are equal or unequal.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "10", "text": "4. Equivalence and Comparison of Fractions: Learners compare fractions by finding common denominators or using visual fraction models.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "11", "text": "5. Arithmetic Expressions Involving Addition and Subtraction of Fractions: Learners apply arithmetic to add and subtract fractions to determine equality.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "12", "text": "6. Applying Properties of Operations: Learners apply properties of operations (commutative, associative, and distributive) to determine equality or inequality.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "13", "text": "Knowledge: Explain what a variable is and its usage in a given expression or equation.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "14", "text": "Skills: Create story problems involving open sentences (equations with variables) in all four operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division) using whole numbers.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "15", "text": "Skills: Solve for the unknown variable in the open sentences derived from story problems using appropriate strategies.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "16", "text": "Learners are expected to: Knowledge 1. Write numeric expressions involving whole-number exponents. 2. Evaluate numeric expressions involving whole-number exponents. Skills 3. Apply properties of operations (commutative, associative, and distributive) to generate equivalent expressions.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "17", "text": "Learners are expected to: Skills 1. Collect and organize data into tables to identify number patterns", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "18", "text": "2. Create graphs (bar graphs, line graphs) to identify and interpret number patterns.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "19", "text": "3. Analyse patterns in tables and graphs to make predictions about future values.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "20", "text": "4. Apply the order of operations in complex expressions involving multiple operations and exponents.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "21", "text": "5. Substitute specific values for variables in algebraic expressions and evaluate the resulting expressions.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "22", "text": "6. Interpret and evaluate expressions that arise from real-world problems, such as those involving distance, time, and rate.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "23", "text": "7. Create real life situations that can be written as expressions.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "24", "text": "Learners are expected to: Knowledge 1. Identify and define independent and dependent variables in real-world problems.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "25", "text": "2. Use variables to represent two quantities that change in relation to one another", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "26", "text": "3. Observe patterns in tables that show how changes in the independent variable affect the dependent variable.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "27", "text": "Skills 4. Plot the values from the table on a coordinate grid to create a graph that shows the relationship between the independent and dependent variables.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "28", "text": "5. Analyse graphs to determine the nature of the relationship (e.g. increasing or decreasing).", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "29", "text": "Values 6. Create stories to explain the patterns in different graphs.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "30", "text": "a. Graphing the Relationship: Learners can create a graph with water on the x-axis and plant height on the y-axis. Each point represents a pair of values from their table. Encourage learners to draw the line of best fit, which represents the equation.", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "31", "text": "b. Using Tables: Learners can extend their table by predicting heights for additional water amounts based on the equation (e.g., 2.5 litres).", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}, {"key": "32", "text": "4. Relating to the Equation: Discuss how the slope of the line indicates how much the height increases with each additional litre of water. Have learners analyse what the y-intercept (10) represents in the context of the problem (the height of the plant when no water is given).", "checked": false, "checked_at": null}]

students.accommodations: Preferential seating near the front of the room; extra time on written assessments.
students.iep_notes: Mild hearing loss in left ear. Benefits from clear articulation and visual cues when new vocabulary is introduced.

students.accommodations: Chunked instructions with visual checklists; scheduled movement breaks every 15 minutes.
students.iep_notes: Diagnosed with ADHD. Focuses best on hands-on tasks and when transitions are signalled in advance.

students.accommodations: Decodable text, reduced text density, and audio support for long passages.
students.iep_notes: Dyslexia - strong oral comprehension but decoding lags one grade level behind. Prefers sans-serif fonts and wider line spacing.

students.accommodations: Sentence starters for discussion; peer support during group work; bilingual glossary allowed.
students.iep_notes: ELL student from a French-Creole home. Building academic English vocabulary; comfortable speaking but cautious when writing.

classes.description: Primary introduction cohort - emphasis on foundational literacy and numeracy through play-based learning.
Consolidation of early literacy and numeracy; building reading fluency and addition/subtraction with regrouping.
Intermediate cohort working toward multiplicative thinking, fractions, and extended informational reading.

config.subject: Mathematics, Language Arts
config.customLearningStyles: Short attention spans - lessons need to alternate between quiet and active segments every 10-15 minutes.
config.specialNeedsDetails: 1 student with mild hearing impairment - benefits from preferential seating near the teacher and clear articulation.
config.culturallyResponsiveNotes: Caribbean / OECS context - use local examples (mango, dasheen, sea grapes) in word problems.
config.behaviorSupportFocus: Positive reinforcement; quiet signals for transitions.
config.availableMaterials: Counting blocks, picture cards, whiteboards, chart paper, Chromebook cart (shared with Grade 2).
config.prerequisiteSkills: Number recognition to 10; phonemic awareness of most consonant sounds.
config.additionalInstructions: Always include a short movement break or song transition halfway through the lesson.

config.subject: Mathematics, Science
config.customLearningStyles: Class works well in pairs; several students are emerging readers who benefit from repeated exposure to new vocabulary.
config.specialNeedsDetails: 1 student with ADHD - benefits from chunked instructions, visual checklists, and scheduled movement breaks.
config.culturallyResponsiveNotes: Incorporate Saint Lucian folk stories, Caribbean geography, and local ecosystems (mangroves, coral reefs, tropical forests) in Science lessons.
config.behaviorSupportFocus: Clear visual schedule posted; timer-based work blocks.
config.availableMaterials: Base-ten blocks, leveled readers, pocket chart, markers, Chromebook cart (shared with Grade 1).
config.prerequisiteSkills: Addition and subtraction within 20; CVC word reading; basic sentence writing.
config.additionalInstructions: Favor partner/share activities. Keep written instructions short and pair them with an icon.

config.subject: Mathematics, Language Arts, Science
config.customLearningStyles: Mixed-ability class - pair differentiated tasks with scaffolds for lower readers and extension questions for advanced learners.
config.specialNeedsDetails: 1 student with dyslexia - needs decodable / reduced-density text and audio support on long passages.
config.culturallyResponsiveNotes: Integrate Caribbean history (slavery, emancipation, independence) and Saint Lucia's geography when relevant.
config.behaviorSupportFocus: Collaborative norms; restorative conversations when conflicts arise.
config.availableMaterials: Fraction tiles, rulers, atlases, leveled chapter books, dedicated Chromebook cart, smartboard, science lab equipment (magnifying glasses, scales, thermometers).
config.prerequisiteSkills: Multiplication facts to 10x10; paragraph writing; map-reading with a key.
config.additionalInstructions: When generating text-heavy content, also supply a simplified / decodable version for the dyslexic student and a vocabulary list for the ELL student.
